paper_title: Aerobiological Pollen Measurements and Study Area Analysis

methods.description: This method involves the collection and analysis of pollen data using gravimetric pollen traps to measure cumulative weekly pollen deposition and transport during the flowering period of ash trees.

steps.description: Install self-constructed gravimetric pollen traps of the Durham type in the selected survey areas.
steps.searchable_summary: install gravimetric pollen traps for data collection

steps.description: Attach five Vaseline coated microscope slides to each trap (one at each cardinal direction and one horizontally) under a rain shelter.
steps.searchable_summary: attach microscope slides to pollen traps

steps.description: Install additional pollen traps at 1.5 m a.g.l. along dominant wind directions to assess horizontal dispersion.
steps.searchable_summary: install additional pollen traps for horizontal dispersion assessment

steps.description: Install additional pollen traps at a height of 5 m a.g.l. to understand vertical pollen distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: install pollen traps for vertical distribution analysis

steps.description: Replace microscopic slides on a weekly basis to analyze pollen quantity with a seven-day temporal resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: replace slides weekly for temporal resolution analysis

steps.description: Stain pollen on slides with gelatin gelvatol solution containing safranin and fix with a coverslip.
steps.searchable_summary: stain and fix pollen on slides

steps.description: Protect prepared slides with clear varnish and analyze using a light microscope at 400× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stained slides with light microscope

steps.description: Count ash pollen along four longitudinal transects and convert to pollen grains per square centimeter.
Analyze spatial and temporal variations in pollen distribution using 'weekly pollen catch' and 'total pollen catch'.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze spatial and temporal pollen distribution

steps.description: Visually delineate the main pollen season and define pre- and post-seasons based on pollen catch data.
